_meta.notes: The Coral Anchorage's signature recurring halfling. Captain of the four-ribbon flagship. Has not lost a contract in nine years. Smiles like she means it; doesn't.

tagline: barefoot, salt-cured, smaller than you expect, dangerous in a way the size makes worse.

behavior.default: stands at the rail and watches the horizon; does not turn for greetings.
narrativeHooks: Eats only at dawn. Will not negotiate at any other hour.
Refuses contracts that target halfling shipping. Everyone else is on the table.
Has not raised her voice on deck in nine years.

trashTalk: the four ribbons mean what they mean.
i have time. you do not.
dueling.refusalLine: not at this hour. not for free.

firstMeet.subject: the four ribbons mean what they mean
firstMeet.body:
You stood on the dock long enough that one of my crew came down the gangway to ask after you. I did not need to ask after you. I had already seen how you stood. 

A contract is not the only kind of conversation a captain has. Sometimes a captain simply wishes a stranger to know they were noticed. You were noticed. The ribbons on the foremast are not decoration; you understand this better than most.

If you return to the Anchorage, return at dawn. We eat then.

— Mira

metadata.voice: clipped, salt-cured, full of nautical idiom; smiles a lot — most mean nothing
